case.paper_title: A Business by Any Other Name: Firm Name Choice as Product Differentiation
case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

original_datasets.name: final_data
original_datasets.columns: [firm_id, chicago, complaints_2008, num_names_2008, emp_size, ad_spending, firm_age, fixedfee, hourlyrate, emergency, multiple_names, ad_spend_k, first_A, deflated_complaints, has_fixed_fee, total_price, in_survey, emergency_A, on_google]

summary_statistics.info: 2293 rows, 19 columns. firm_id: object; others float32. Key variables: complaints_2008 (count), first_A (indicator), multiple_names, on_google, ad_spend_k, firm_age, chicago, emp_size.

original_datasets.notes: Data appear to include Illinois plumbing firms with overall complaint counts. No additional filtering required for Task2 per provided instructions.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Load final_data.dta. Explore distribution, create complaints_binary, run chi-square test, fit Poisson and negative binomial models for complaints_2008 ~ first_A, compare fit, optionally fit zero-inflated models, and fit NB with controls.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: first_A (indicator if firm name begins with 'A' or a number).
key_choices.outlier_rule: Dichotomized robustness check using complaints_binary with chi-square test.
key_choices.data_processing: Construct complaints_binary for robustness; no additional recoding.
key_choices.model_choice: Negative binomial regression; Poisson and zero-inflated models used for model fit assessment; Welch t-test as descriptive robustness check.
key_choices.inference_rule: Significance of coefficient for first_A (z-test) in negative binomial regression.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of complaints recorded in 2008 for each firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator equals 1 if firm name begins with 'A' or a number; 0 otherwise.

controls.name: multiple_names
controls.definition: Indicator for whether the firm uses multiple names.

controls.name: on_google
controls.definition: Indicator for whether the firm advertises on Google.

controls.name: firm_age
controls.definition: Age of the firm in years.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the provided R scripts. Reads /app/data/final_data.dta and runs chi-square, Poisson, NB, zero-inflated, NB with controls, and t-test.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Use only Illinois Plumbing Firms. Do not restrict to metro Chicago. Do not use Yelp/Angie's List/Checkbook reviews. Use overall complaint counts (not per employee).

analysis_path.path_description: Use the same data file and fit negative binomial regression of complaints_2008 on first_A with controls (multiple_names, on_google, ad_spend_k, firm_age, chicago, emp_size).
preprocessing_steps: No restriction to metro Chicago; include chicago as a control.

key_choices.sample_restriction: None beyond Illinois firms in final_data.dta.
key_choices.data_processing: No additional processing beyond variable selection.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of complaints recorded in 2008 for each firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator equals 1 if firm name begins with 'A' or a number; 0 otherwise.

controls.name: multiple_names
controls.definition: Indicator for whether the firm uses multiple names.

controls.name: on_google
controls.definition: Indicator for whether the firm advertises on Google.

controls.name: firm_age
controls.definition: Age of the firm in years.

analysis_code.notes: Same Python script executes the negative binomial model with controls that satisfies Task2 instructions.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned negative binomial regression with controls; do not include review-based variables and do not subselect metro Chicago only.

files.data/McDevitt_JournPoliEco_2014_yQeR--AN: Python translation of the R analyses. Loads /app/data/final_data.dta, computes chi-square on complaints_binary, fits Poisson and negative binomial models, compares fit, optionally fits zero-inflated models, fits NB with controls, and runs a Welch t-test.

codebase.notes: Original R scripts are present but the execution entrypoint is the Python translation to comply with the run policy. Ensure the dataset is mounted to /app/data inside the container.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset final_data.dta and the Python rewrite to produce standardized outputs and model summaries.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Negative binomial coefficient for first_A (estimate, z, p); Poisson vs NB LLR and p; chi-square test for complaints_binary; Welch t-test for mean complaints by first_A; NB with controls summary.
